$comment: FC17.9(c) (D-CSUBSET-SETJMP + D-OPT-SETJMP-RETURNS-TWICE-INLINE) - C11 <setjmp.h> setjmp/longjmp on 4 legs / 3 realizations, RUN on BOTH the baseline (debug) AND the `release` pipeline (Mem2Reg + Inlining active). elf x86_64 + arm64 (qemu) resolve setjmp/longjmp as DIRECT libc.so.6 FFI; pe64 expands `setjmp(env)` via the pe-only descriptor macro to msvcrt's `_setjmp(env, 0)` (frame=0 non-SEH restore) + DIRECT `longjmp`; macho arm64 is DIRECT libSystem FFI (RUNS natively on a macOS-arm64 CI host). `run` (a callee of main) does the classic round-trip; `longjmp` is noreturn so the post-longjmp path is Unreachable. The release arm is the load-bearing proof of the optimizer hardening: mem2reg must NOT promote `modified` (a local stored before longjmp and read after the resume — a promoted read would observe the stale entry value 0 -> exit 40; kept in memory -> exit 42), and the inliner must NOT inline the returns-twice callee `run`. `determinate` (computed before setjmp, unmodified) is the M1 Call-barrier + longjmp-restore witness. exit 40 (determinate) + 2 (modified) = 42. ANTI-FOLD: g_base/g_bump are mutable globals so no pass folds the round-trip to `return 42`. RED-on-disable (mem2reg): revert the returns-twice whole-function bail in src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp -> the release arm exits 40 not 42. RED-on-disable (header): delete shippedLibs/setjmp.json -> `#include <setjmp.h>` fires F_ShippedHeaderNotFound and the compile fails.
